name: My ears are ringing after years at the factory, can I file a workers comp claim for hearing loss?

mainEntity.name: My ears are ringing after years at the factory, can I file a workers comp claim for hearing loss?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, file workers' comp for work-related hearing loss with audiogram proof and exposure records; Utah/Idaho recognize it as occupational disease.[1][3]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can file a workers' compensation claim for hearing loss caused by prolonged workplace noise exposure if you can prove it was work-related. In Utah and Idaho, noise-induced hearing loss is recognized as an occupational disease eligible for benefits like medical costs and disability payments.[1][3][5] Context: Employers must follow OSHA standards for noise above 85 decibels, providing protection and testing; failure to do so supports your claim.[6] Key factors: Medical evidence from audiograms linking loss to job noise, documentation of exposure levels, and employer negligence like no ear protection.[1][2] Practical guidance: Get an immediate audiogram, notify your employer in writing, and file within 1 year of last exposure in Utah or per Idaho rules; consult a workers' comp attorney to build evidence.[3][7] Many succeed with proper proof despite delays.

description: Yes, file workers' comp for work-related hearing loss with audiogram proof and exposure records; Utah/Idaho recognize it as occupational disease.[1][3]
